title: tetramolopium consanguineum subsp. consanguineum
intro: Tetramolopium consanguineum subsp. consanguineum, also known as the 'Fijian Rosella', is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. The plant is traditionally used in Pacific Island medicine, particularly in Fijian and Tongan herbalism, for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The Fijian Rosella is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and relieving respiratory issues, among others. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and poultices to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
